source: Greenbarz · Curated · Brent / Dubai / BFOET basket
note: Baseline values are typical-relationship placeholders. Replace from GX terminal during next scrape session — search 'BFOET grades' or each individual code on GX.

grades.name: Brent (Dated)
grades.role: Global benchmark · ~70% of seaborne crude pricing
grades.context: The world's most-watched crude marker. Dated Brent is the daily-assessed cash market for North Sea crude delivered FOB Sullom Voe — the underlying that prices most international long-haul barrels. Spread to WTI reflects geopolitical risk premium + Atlantic Basin demand.

grades.name: Oseberg
grades.role: BFOET basket grade · Norwegian medium sweet
grades.context: Equinor-operated, eligible for delivery into Dated Brent. Slightly heavier than Brent itself (37° API vs 38°). Flow ~150-200 kb/d. Premium/discount vs Dated Brent reflects refiner appetite for medium-sweet vs lighter grades — driving season pulls Oseberg above Brent.

grades.name: Ekofisk
grades.role: BFOET basket grade · Light sweet, oldest North Sea field
grades.context: ConocoPhillips-operated, ~38° API, delivered Teesside. The lightest of the BFOET grades and usually trades at a small premium to Dated Brent — preferred by European gasoline-skewed refiners. Production ~280 kb/d.

grades.name: Troll
grades.role: BFOET basket grade · Premium light sweet
grades.context: Equinor-operated, ~36° API, delivered Mongstad. Highest-quality BFOET grade and usually the most expensive — strong distillate yield. Often the price-setter for the basket when North Sea supply tightens. Production ~330 kb/d.

grades.name: Johan Sverdrup
grades.role: BFOET basket grade · Newest field · Medium heavy sour
grades.context: Norway's largest field, Equinor-operated. ~28° API, ~0.8% sulfur — heavier and more sour than the rest of BFOET. Trades at a notable discount to Dated Brent (~$2-4/bbl) reflecting lower yield of premium products. Production ~720 kb/d, the basket's largest single grade by volume.

grades.name: Dubai
grades.role: Asian benchmark · prices Mideast → Asia flows
grades.context: Platts-assessed, the Asian counterpart to Brent. About 25 mb/d of crude (most Mideast Gulf grades) priced off Dubai. Brent-Dubai spread (EFS) is the key arb signal between Atlantic and Pacific basins — narrow EFS = Asia bidding aggressively for North Sea barrels.

grades.name: Murban
grades.role: Light sour Mideast benchmark · ICE Murban futures
grades.context: ADNOC's flagship grade, ~40° API, ~0.8% sulfur. Now has its own ICE futures contract (since 2021), trades as a lighter alternative to Dubai. Premium to Dubai reflects higher distillate yield. Most Asian refiners take Murban via long-term term contracts.

grades.name: ESPO Blend
grades.context: Eastern Siberia-Pacific Ocean pipeline blend, delivered Kozmino port. ~35° API, low sulfur. Heavily discounted (~$15-20/bbl below Brent) post-2022 sanctions — buyers are mostly Chinese teapots + Indian state refiners. Spread vs Brent is the cleanest signal of Russia sanction enforcement intensity.

grades.name: Basrah Medium
grades.context: SOMO-marketed Iraqi grade, ~30° API, ~2.7% sulfur. Iraq is OPEC's #2 producer and Basrah Medium is its main export grade. Term-contract pricing tied to Dubai. Watch the Basrah-Dubai differential for Iraqi production discipline signals.
